evals.prompt: I want to add BarcodeSelection to my Kotlin Multiplatform app so users can tap to select individual barcodes in a dense scene. Here's my empty shared screen model: EmptyScreenModel.kt. I need to select EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes, shared between Android and iOS.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md and writes a complete BarcodeSelection integration into EmptyScreenModel.kt: DataCaptureContext.initialize with a license key placeholder, BarcodeSelection.forContext with BarcodeSelectionSettings.barcodeSelectionSettings() enabling EAN13_UPCA and CODE128 and selectionType set to BarcodeSelectionTapSelection.tapSelection(), a Camera wired via setFrameSource, a BarcodeSelectionListener implementation, and a setupDataCaptureView function the platform hosts call. It also explains that Android and iOS each construct their own DataCaptureView (different constructor signatures) and embed it via toAndroidView()/toUIView().

assertions.text: com.kmp.datacapture.barcode.selection.BarcodeSelection is imported (not com.scandit.datacapture.*)
A license key placeholder string is present (e.g. -- ENTER YOUR SCANDIT LICENSE KEY HERE --)
DataCaptureContext.initialize( is used to create the context
BarcodeSelection.createRecommendedCameraSettings() is used to configure the camera
Camera.getDefaultCamera( is used to obtain the camera
dataCaptureContext.setFrameSource( is called with the camera
BarcodeSelectionSettings.barcodeSelectionSettings() factory is used (not a bare BarcodeSelectionSettings() constructor)
settings.selectionType is set to BarcodeSelectionTapSelection.tapSelection()
Symbology.EAN13_UPCA is enabled
Symbology.CODE128 is enabled
BarcodeSelection.forContext(dataCaptureContext, settings) is used to create the mode (not forDataCaptureContext)
addListener( is called or equivalent listener registration
The screen model implements BarcodeSelectionListener with onSelectionUpdated and onSessionUpdated
onSelectionUpdated reads from session.newlySelectedBarcodes (a list, not a single nullable barcode)
A setupDataCaptureView (or equivalently named) function accepts a DataCaptureView and attaches a BarcodeSelectionBasicOverlay via withBarcodeSelectionForView or addOverlay
The response explains that Android's DataCaptureView constructor needs a Context while iOS's does not, so the view must be constructed on each platform host rather than in the shared screen model

evals.prompt: In my KMP BarcodeSelection screen model (IntegratedScreenModel.kt), I want users to aim at a barcode instead of tapping it, and I want it selected automatically as soon as the SDK understands the user is aiming at it — no extra tap required. Switch the selection strategy accordingly.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets settings.selectionType to BarcodeSelectionAimerSelection.aimerSelection() with selectionStrategy set to BarcodeSelectionAutoSelectionStrategy(), and reapplies the settings via barcodeSelection.applySettings(settings). Existing integration code is preserved.

assertions.text: BarcodeSelectionAimerSelection.aimerSelection() is used to construct the aimer selection type
selectionStrategy is set to BarcodeSelectionAutoSelectionStrategy()
settings.selectionType is assigned the constructed aimer selection instance
barcodeSelection.applySettings(settings) is called to apply the change at runtime
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeSelection, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Users tell me tapping the same barcode twice in my KMP BarcodeSelection app (IntegratedScreenModel.kt) accidentally unselects it. I want repeated taps on an already-selected barcode to just increase a count instead of unselecting it, and I want to show that count in the UI when a barcode is (re-)selected.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets settings.tapBehavior (on the BarcodeSelectionTapSelection instance) to BarcodeSelectionTapBehavior.REPEAT_SELECTION, and in onSelectionUpdated reads session.getCount(barcode) for barcodes in session.newlySelectedBarcodes to surface the times-selected count in the UI. Existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: tapBehavior is set to BarcodeSelectionTapBehavior.REPEAT_SELECTION on the BarcodeSelectionTapSelection instance
onSelectionUpdated reads session.newlySelectedBarcodes to get newly selected barcodes
session.getCount(barcode) is called to read how many times the barcode was selected
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeSelection, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my KMP BarcodeSelection app (IntegratedScreenModel.kt), I want a button that freezes the camera preview so the user can carefully tap several barcodes without them moving, and another button to resume live scanning.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds functions or event handling that call barcodeSelection.freezeCamera() to freeze and barcodeSelection.unfreezeCamera() to resume. Existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: barcodeSelection.freezeCamera() is called to freeze the preview
barcodeSelection.unfreezeCamera() is called to resume the preview
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeSelection, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my KMP BarcodeSelection app (IntegratedScreenModel.kt), most scenes only ever have a single barcode in view. When that's the case, I want that barcode selected automatically without the user having to tap or aim at it.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets settings.singleBarcodeAutoDetection = true on the BarcodeSelectionSettings, before mode creation or via applySettings. Existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: settings.singleBarcodeAutoDetection is set to true
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeSelection, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want silent selection in my KMP BarcodeSelection app — suppress the default beep when a barcode is selected, no vibration either. Update IntegratedScreenModel.kt.
evals.expected_output: The skill calls barcodeSelection.setFeedback(soundEnabled = false, vibrationEnabled = false), or assigns barcodeSelection.feedback to a fresh BarcodeSelectionFeedback()/a Feedback with sound and vibration both null. Existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: barcodeSelection.setFeedback(soundEnabled = false, vibrationEnabled = false) is called, or barcodeSelection.feedback is reassigned to suppress both sound and vibration (not BarcodeSelectionFeedback.defaultFeedback())
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeSelection, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: My KMP BarcodeSelection screen model (IntegratedScreenModel.kt) doesn't clean up properly when the screen is left. Add a dispose function that removes the listener, removes the mode from the context, and turns off the camera.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds a dispose() function that sets barcodeSelection.isEnabled = false, calls barcodeSelection.removeListener(this), calls dataCaptureContext.removeMode(barcodeSelection), and calls camera?.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.OFF), in that order. Existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: barcodeSelection.removeListener( is called in the new dispose function
dataCaptureContext.removeMode(barcodeSelection) is called (not removeCurrentMode or removeAllModes)
camera?.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.OFF) (or non-null equivalent) is called to turn off the camera
barcodeSelection.isEnabled = false is set before removing the listener
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeSelection, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to use Compose Multiplatform for my KMP BarcodeSelection screen instead of the imperative shared-ScreenModel pattern. Here's my empty Compose screen: EmptyComposeScreen.kt. Let users tap to select EAN-13 and QR barcodes.
evals.expected_output: The skill uses the core-compose DataCaptureView composable: rememberDataCaptureContext(licenseKey) to create the context, rememberCamera(context) to wire and drive the camera, BarcodeSelection.forContext with BarcodeSelectionSettings.barcodeSelectionSettings() enabling EAN13_UPCA and QR, a remembered BarcodeSelectionBasicOverlay.withBarcodeSelection(barcodeSelection), and passes overlays = listOf(overlay) into the DataCaptureView composable. It does not look for a BarcodeSelectionView composable (none exists) and flags that the overlay must be remember-ed to avoid recomposition churn.

assertions.text: rememberDataCaptureContext( is used to create the DataCaptureContext
rememberCamera( is used to wire the camera
BarcodeSelection.forContext( is used to create the mode
BarcodeSelectionSettings.barcodeSelectionSettings() is used
Symbology.EAN13_UPCA is enabled
Symbology.QR is enabled
BarcodeSelectionBasicOverlay.withBarcodeSelection( is used (not withBarcodeSelectionForView, since there is no imperative DataCaptureView to bind to)
The overlay instance is wrapped in remember( to keep it stable across recompositions
The DataCaptureView composable is called with overlays = listOf(overlay)
No BarcodeSelectionView composable is used or suggested, since BarcodeSelection has no dedicated Compose companion
